comparison.title: Clinical Comparison: Electrolysis vs. Laser
comparison.description: Understanding the fundamental biological difference between permanent removal and temporary reduction.

electrolysis.title: Electrolysis
electrolysis.subtitle: The Gold Standard
electrolysis.description: The only FDA-approved method for truly permanent hair removal. Works by destroying the follicle growth center.

laser.title: Laser Hair Removal
laser.subtitle: Temporary Reduction
laser.description: A popular method that uses light energy to damage follicles. Requires lifelong maintenance sessions.

comparison.quote: "While Laser Hair Removal offers temporary reduction, it does not permanently stop hair growth. Electrolysis remains the only medical solution for 100% hair-free results across all biological variations."

hero.title: Permanent hair removal, medically proven.
hero.description: Electrolysis is the only FDA-approved method for permanent hair removal. Our certified specialists use medical-grade technology for safe, effective treatment on all skin types and hair colors.

whyElectrolysis.title: Why Electrolysis is the Only Permanent Solution
whyElectrolysis.description: Electrolysis is the FDA-approved method for permanent hair removal. Unlike temporary methods, electrolysis destroys the hair follicle completely.

methods.name: Electrolysis
methods.description: Destroys hair at the root permanently

explainer.title: The Permanent Difference
explainer.paragraph1: Electrolysis works by sending a slight electrical current through a fine probe inserted into each hair follicle. This destroys the follicle's ability to produce hair permanently. Once destroyed, that follicle will never regrow hair, not in weeks, months, or years.
explainer.paragraph2: This is why electrolysis is the only FDA-approved permanent hair removal method. While other methods may reduce hair, only electrolysis offers true permanence.

benefits.title: Benefits of Permanent Hair Removal
benefits.description: Discover why thousands choose electrolysis for lasting results and medical-grade safety.

items.title: Truly Permanent
items.description: Once a hair follicle is treated, it's gone forever. No more hair in that area.

items.title: All Skin Types
items.description: Works effectively on all skin tones, from very fair to very dark skin.

items.title: All Hair Colors
items.description: Effective on blonde, red, white, and dark hair, even color that laser can't treat.

items.title: Precise Control
items.description: Target individual hairs with surgical precision. Ideal for facial hair and sensitive areas.

items.title: Medically Safe
items.description: FDA-approved, no UV radiation, minimal risk of side effects when done by certified professionals.

items.title: Long-Term Savings
items.description: While initial cost is higher, permanent results mean no more ongoing treatments or products.

science.title: Science-Based Results
items: No more daily hair removal routines
Say goodbye to razor burns and ingrown hairs
Ideal for bikini line, face, and sensitive areas
Treatment customized to your hair growth cycle

hairGrowthPhases.title: Understanding Hair Growth Cycles
hairGrowthPhases.description: Hair doesn't grow continuously. Understanding these three phases explains why permanent hair removal requires multiple treatments.
hairGrowthPhases.treatableNote: Only treatment during Anagen results in permanent hair removal.

phases.name: Anagen
phases.subtitle: Growth Phase
phases.description: Hair actively grows from the follicle. This is the only phase when electrolysis can permanently destroy the follicle.
details: Hair is thickest and most pigmented
Requires direct treatment to be permanent

phases.name: Catagen
phases.subtitle: Transition Phase
phases.description: Hair transitions from growth to rest. The follicle begins to separate from the dermal papilla.

phases.name: Telogen
phases.subtitle: Resting Phase
phases.description: Hair rests in the follicle before being shed. The follicle is dormant and cannot be treated.
details: Hair is no longer attached to root
Cannot be treated during this phase

multiple.title: Why Multiple Treatments Are Needed
multiple.paragraph1: At any given time, only about 85% of hair is in the Anagen growth phase. The remaining 10-15% is in Catagen or Telogen phases and cannot be treated. This means your first appointment can only treat the actively growing hairs.
multiple.paragraph2: Over the next several weeks, new hairs from Catagen and Telogen phases will enter Anagen and become visible. These are treated in follow-up sessions until all hairs have been permanently removed. This typically takes 6-12 months depending on body area and individual hair growth patterns.

steps.label: First Treatment
steps.desc: Treats 85% of hairs in Anagen phase

steps.label: Follow-up Sessions
steps.desc: Treats newly visible hairs from other phases

steps.label: Permanent Result
steps.desc: No more hair growth once complete

howItWorks.title: How Electrolysis Works
howItWorks.description: Step-by-step explanation of our treatment process and what to expect during your appointment.

steps.title: Free Consultation
steps.description: Meet with our certified electrologist to discuss your hair removal goals, skin type, and treatment expectations. We'll answer all your questions.

steps.title: Skin Preparation
steps.description: We cleanse and prepare your skin. You may apply numbing cream if desired. The area is made comfortable for treatment.

steps.title: Hair Treatment
steps.description: A fine probe is gently inserted into each hair follicle. A quick electrical current destroys the follicle permanently. The probe never pierces the skin.

steps.title: Skin Soothing
steps.description: After treatment, we apply soothing products to reduce any redness. Most clients see minimal to no visible reaction.

science.title: The Science Behind Electrolysis
science.methodDescription: A direct electrical current creates a chemical reaction that destroys the follicle. This method is particularly effective and causes minimal discomfort.
science.permanentDescription: The electrical current destroys the dermal papilla and matrix cells, the biological structures responsible for hair production. Once destroyed, these cannot regenerate.

items.desc: Keep treated area protected for 48 hours
Let skin heal naturally, don't pick at hairs
Avoid hot showers and strenuous exercise

faq.title: Everything you need to know about electrolysis.
faq.description: Answers to common questions about the treatment process, results, and permanent hair removal.

items.question: Is electrolysis really permanent?
items.answer: Yes. Electrolysis is FDA-approved as the only permanent method of hair removal. Once the hair follicle is treated and destroyed, it cannot produce hair again. Results are permanent.

items.question: How long does electrolysis take?
items.answer: Treatment time varies based on the area and hair density. Small areas like the upper lip may take 15-30 minutes, while larger areas can take 1-2 hours. Your electrologist will provide a timeline at your consultation.

items.question: How many sessions will I need?
items.answer: Most clients need 6-12 sessions spread over 6-12 months. This is because only hair in the active growth phase can be treated permanently. Spacing allows new hairs to enter the growth phase.

items.question: Does electrolysis hurt?
items.answer: Electrolysis causes minimal discomfort. Most clients describe it as slight tingling or warmth. Numbing cream is available if desired. The sensation varies by individual tolerance and area being treated.

items.question: Will there be redness or marks after treatment?
items.answer: Most clients experience minimal redness that fades within hours to a few days. Some may see slight marks that disappear quickly. We apply soothing products immediately after treatment to minimize any reaction.

items.question: Can electrolysis work on all skin types and hair colors?
items.answer: Yes. Unlike laser hair removal, electrolysis works on all skin types and all hair colors, including blonde, red, gray, and white. This makes it the most inclusive permanent hair removal method.
